mano_cua.result_summary: Bug confirmed via source code diff. In body.component.ts, @ViewChild(ScrollerComponent, { static: true }) should be { static: false }. With static:true, Angular resolves the ViewChild in ngOnInit before change detection, causing scroller to be undefined when virtual scrolling is conditionally rendered. This breaks virtual paging — scroll events are not captured, so page changes never fire. Fix changes to { static: false }.
mano_cua.last_reasoning: Single-line fix in src/components/body/body.component.ts line 241. Buggy: @ViewChild(ScrollerComponent, { static: true }). Fixed: @ViewChild(ScrollerComponent, { static: false }). Angular's static:true resolves before ngAfterViewInit, meaning dynamically rendered ScrollerComponent is undefined at init time.
